claim: A national hospital readmission reduction programme cut 30-day all-cause readmission rates from 15.8% to 11.2% over three years, saving an confirmed $890M in Medicare expenditure.

corpus.title: National Readmission Reduction Initiative — 3-Year Outcome Report
corpus.source: Centre for Hospital Quality Improvement / Department of Health and Human Services
corpus.snippet: A national hospital readmission reduction programme cut 30-day all-cause readmission rates from 15.8% to 11.2% over thre...
corpus.content:
CENTRE FOR HOSPITAL QUALITY IMPROVEMENT
National Readmission Reduction Initiative (NRRI) — 3-Year Outcome Report
Published: March 1, 2024

PROGRAMME BACKGROUND
The NRRI enrolled 4,218 acute care hospitals beginning January 2021, implementing structured care transition protocols including: pharmacist-led medication reconciliation, nurse navigator post-discharge calls (Days 2, 7, and 14), and primary care appointment scheduling within 7 days of discharge.

PRIMARY OUTCOME — 30-DAY ALL-CAUSE READMISSION RATE
Baseline (calendar year 2020): 15.8%
Year 1 (2021):                 14.4%
Year 2 (2022):                 12.6%
Year 3 (2023):                 11.2%
Total reduction:               -4.6 percentage points (-29.1%)

FINANCIAL IMPACT (Medicare Fee-for-Service claims analysis)
Averted readmissions (2021-2023): approximately 218,000 hospitalisation episodes
Estimated gross Medicare savings: $890M over 3 years
Programme implementation cost (federal grant): $124M
Net savings: $766M

CONDITION-SPECIFIC REDUCTIONS:
  Heart failure: 15.8% → 9.7% (-38.6%)
  Pneumonia: 14.1% → 10.4% (-26.2%)
  COPD: 20.2% → 15.8% (-21.8%)

Participating hospitals with ≥80% protocol adherence achieved 2× the readmission reduction compared to low-adherence sites.

corpus.title: Report: Centre for Hospital Quality Improvement — Distorted Account
corpus.snippet: A national hospital readmission reduction programme cut 30-day all-cause readmission rates from 15.8...
corpus.content:
Breaking: Centre for Hospital Quality Improvement shocks the industry amid growing scrutiny in the healthcare sector

A national hospital readmission reduction programme cut 30-day all-cause readmission rates from 15.8% to 11.2% over three years, saving an confirmed $890M in Medicare expenditure. According to sources familiar with the matter, the decision follows months of internal deliberation. Industry analysts noted the move signals a broader shift in healthcare strategy. A spokesperson for Centre for Hospital Quality Improvement declined to comment beyond the official statement.

corpus.title: News: Centre for Hospital Quality Improvement — Accurate Report
corpus.snippet: A national hospital readmission reduction programme cut 30-day all-cause readmission rates from 15.8...
corpus.content:
Breaking: Centre for Hospital Quality Improvement makes a significant move amid changes in the healthcare sector

A national hospital readmission reduction programme cut 30-day all-cause readmission rates from 15.8% to 11.2% over three years, saving an estimated $890M in Medicare expenditure. According to sources familiar with the matter, the decision follows months of internal deliberation. Industry analysts noted the move signals a broader shift in healthcare strategy. A spokesperson for Centre for Hospital Quality Improvement declined to comment beyond the official statement.
